items.text:
Subject: Spring Photography Club Outdoor Exhibition — Sign Up by April 20

Dear Members,

The Spring Photography Club is hosting our annual outdoor exhibition on Saturday, May 4 in Founders Plaza, from 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM. This year's theme is 'Hidden Corners of Campus,' and we welcome both members and non-members to submit up to three photographs.

A $5 participation fee covers display materials, refreshments, and a printed program. Members who help set up tables before 9:00 AM will receive complimentary entry. Please email your high-resolution images to spc-exhibit@university.edu by April 20, along with a one-sentence description for each piece.

If you have a friend who would like to attend but not exhibit, free admission is open to all guests throughout the day. We hope this event encourages more students across departments to share their work and meet other creative people on campus.

Best regards,
Maya Lin
Club Coordinator

format_metadata.subject: Spring Photography Club Outdoor Exhibition — Sign Up by April 20

questions.stem: According to the email, what must members do to receive free entry?

options.B: Help set up tables before 9:00 AM.
options.C: Pay the participation fee in advance.
options.D: Attend the exhibition as a guest only.

questions.explanation: The email states 'Members who help set up tables before 9:00 AM will receive complimentary entry.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about non-members who want to participate?

options.A: They may still submit work for display.
options.B: They will be charged a higher entry fee.
options.C: They are required to exhibit if they attend.
options.D: They cannot enter without member sponsorship.

questions.explanation: The email says 'we welcome both members and non-members to submit up to three photographs.'

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To remind members about overdue club dues.
options.B: To announce a change to a previous schedule.
options.C: To recruit new photographers to club leadership.
options.D: To invite members to participate in an exhibition.

questions.explanation: The email's central purpose is to invite members to submit photos for the May 4 exhibition.

items.text:
Subject: Free Bicycle Maintenance Clinic — Save Your Spot for September 14

Hi everyone,

The Campus Transportation Office and the Bike Co-op are running a free bicycle maintenance clinic on Saturday, September 14 from 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM on the East Lawn. Whether you ride to class every day or only on weekends, we'll show you the basics that keep a bike rolling.

Stations will cover tire pressure and patch repair, brake adjustment, chain cleaning, and gear tuning. Trained student mechanics will work alongside you — we don't fix the bike for you, but you leave knowing how. Bring your own bike if possible; a few loaners will be available on a first-come basis.

You must register online at transport.university.edu/clinic by September 12 so we can prepare enough materials. The clinic is open to all students, staff, and faculty.

Thanks,
Jordan Ruiz
Campus Transportation Office

format_metadata.subject: Free Bicycle Maintenance Clinic — Save Your Spot for September 14

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the deadline to register for the clinic?
questions.explanation: The email says 'register online at transport.university.edu/clinic by September 12.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the role of the student mechanics at the clinic?

options.A: They will fix every bike entirely on their own.
options.B: They will teach attendees rather than do the work.
options.C: They will charge a small fee for their time.
options.D: They will be available only by appointment.

questions.explanation: The email states 'we don't fix the bike for you, but you leave knowing how' — emphasis on teaching, not doing.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To list new safety rules for campus cyclists.
options.B: To explain a new fee for bike registration.
options.C: To invite the campus to a maintenance clinic.
options.D: To announce changes to the existing bike co-op.

questions.explanation: The email primarily invites recipients to attend the free September 14 maintenance clinic.
